$comment: Per-module render meta consumed by assets/render_module.py. 'cite_sentence' carries <!-- cite:N -->[[N]](#ref-N) markers wired to the References; 'references' are full one-line bib entries each ending in a source tag (local:/spec:/web/abstract-only) per .claude/rules/citation-integrity.md. 'order' lists unit keys (filtered to those present); 'titles' maps key -> display label. Optional 'survey' adds a 'Part of the <survey>' note to the module header. This is an illustrative example — the skill decomposes ANY codebase.
title: Text generation — decoding-layer capability tree (`acme.generate`)
coverage: a transformer inference library's public text-generation layer (`acme.generate`)
cite_sentence: Nucleus (top-p) sampling follows Holtzman et al. <!-- cite:1 -->[[1]](#ref-1); top-k truncation follows Fan et al. <!-- cite:2 -->[[2]](#ref-2).
references: [1] Holtzman et al., "The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration," ICLR (2020). [link] (web)
[2] Fan et al., "Hierarchical Neural Story Generation," ACL (2018). [link] (web)
